SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper's main claims (direct NL→SQL models + feedback loop improve performance) are supported by multiple experiments: benchmark evaluations on GEO880 and ATIS, a live three-stage online experiment (reach 63.51% after three stages), and simulated interactive experiments (Figures/Tables showing accuracy and annotation effort). The evaluation metric (execute predicted SQL and compare results to gold) is appropriate. However, some methodological assumptions are optimistic (simulated experiments assume perfect user feedback) and I see no mention of statistical significance testing; these are minor issues that do not invalidate the main conclusions.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper combines neural sequence-to-sequence modeling directly targeting full SQL (rather than simpler logical forms) with an end-to-end feedback-driven deployment loop that leverages cheap crowd annotation of SQL, which extends prior neural semantic parsing work. The authors position this as extending neural approaches to full SQL and deploying a live interactive learning loop (claimed as first such live system), so the contribution is a non-trivial and clearly articulated extension of existing methods rather than a trivial rehash.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is organized into clear sections (Algorithm 1 for the feedback loop, sections on entity anonymization, data augmentation, benchmark and interactive experiments) and states contributions early. Figures/tables are referenced and concrete experimental details (batch sizes, optimizer, epochs, beam size) are given. The provided excerpt contains some repetitive and slightly noisy text fragments, but overall the methodology and experiments are described clearly enough to follow.

SUBSTANCE.justification: The work provides a breadth of evaluations: standard benchmarks (GEO880, ATIS), a live online deployment with user feedback, and simulated interactive experiments analyzing annotation effort (Table 7) and feedback quality (Table 6). They also study data augmentation effects (templates and paraphrasing). Some deeper analyses are limited (no uncertainty estimates/statistics, limited ablations of model internals), but the experimental scope is substantial and practical details for reproduction (hyperparameters, data processing promises) are provided.

IMPACT.justification: The paper tackles an important, practical problem (building NL interfaces to databases) and demonstrates a deployable feedback-driven approach that could change how such systems are bootstrapped for new domains. The direct NL→SQL mapping plus crowd-corrected feedback loop is likely to be useful to practitioners and to open follow-up research on interactive learning and interfaces, though it is not radically transformative by itself.

APPROPRIATENESS.justification: The topic—neural semantic parsing to SQL and interactive learning from user feedback—fits squarely within the scope of top NLP/ML conferences. The problem, methods (seq2seq, attention, data augmentation), and evaluations (benchmarks, live deployment) match community standards and audience interests.

MEANINGFUL_COMPARISON.justification: The paper evaluates on standard datasets (GEO880, ATIS) using standard splits and compares to relevant prior work (Dong & Lapata, Jia & Liang, Poon, etc.), and discusses when templates/paraphrases help or not. While comparative numbers and detailed analysis are referenced, the excerpt shows limited discussion of statistical significance and some baseline details may be omitted, but overall comparisons appear fair and appropriate.
